biography: Dana Ledoux Miller is a Samoan writer, producer, director, and showrunner. She co-wrote and co-directed Moana 2 for Walt Disney Animation Studios and later contributed to the live-action Moana film. She was co-showrunner for Netflix's limited series Thai Cave Rescue, produced by SKG and Jon M. Chu. Previously, she was an executive producer on Last Resort for Seven Bucks Productions and Feigco Entertainment as part of the Break the Room Initiative, which was developed for Quibi. Her writing credits include Kevin Can Fck Himself* and Lodge 49 for AMC, Designated Survivor for ABC, Narcos for Netflix, and The Newsroom for HBO. In addition, she co-founded the non-profit Pasifika Entertainment Advancement Komiti (PEAK). Ledoux Miller is a graduate of the University of Hawai'i at Mānoa.
name: Dana Ledoux Miller
